### SPE-151 - World-law compatibility and contradiction rules
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T10:52:04.755Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement world-law compatibility so local environments can support, suppress, distort, or reject broad classes of tools, powers, entities, and movement modes through explicit domain ceilings.

Scope:

* support multiple environment or domain categories such as technological, spiritual, magical, social, or analogous capability bands
* validate imported items, actors, powers, and actions against local domain support before or during use
* generate explicit contradiction states when an attempted activity exceeds local support, with deterministic enforcement such as degradation, suppression, rerouting, or correction
* support source-type selective suppression and ideology-tag admission where some methods remain valid while others are blocked
* support explicit world-law parameters for gravity, advanced transit, paradox, and other setting assumptions where relevant
* connect the layer to power families, item-runtime, site-state, and jurisdiction systems

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no need for every room to define a unique physics model
* no narrative handwave when incompatibility matters mechanically
* prefer compact domain profiles and contradiction rules over sprawling exception lists
* keep support and contradiction outcomes inspectable enough for authoring review and player explanation

Acceptance criteria:

* at least two domain categories can independently support or reject an imported effect
* at least one unsupported action produces a contradiction state rather than simple failure only
* at least one source-type selective suppression rule distinguishes among ability families
* at least one explicit world-law parameter materially changes downstream behavior
* targeted tests cover domain validation, contradiction generation, and enforcement outcomes

### SPE-144 - Polity-Driven Settlement Generation
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-24T15:43:29.648Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded settlement-seeding layer so villages and towns can be generated from political and technological profile, not just population size.

Scope:

* support generation of defenses, roads, works, service mix, and civic layout from polity profile such as government form and technology level
* let political profile alter what kinds of services, walls, roads, and institutions are plausible in a generated settlement
* connect generated settlements to existing urban service nodes, district encounter scheduling, and regional map systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no full SimCity-style city simulator
* no requirement to generate every building in exhaustive detail
* prefer compact settlement generators keyed by profile over sprawling bespoke civic blueprints
* keep generation outputs legible enough for later authoring refinement

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one settlement layout changes based on political or technological profile rather than population alone
* at least one generated output affects defenses or service mix materially
* targeted tests cover deterministic polity-profile generation and downstream settlement differences

Reconciliation update

Fold externally pressured extraction villages and mixed-population loyalty tension into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some settlements may exist primarily to satisfy outside extraction quotas, taxes, hauling duties, or military demands rather than for internal prosperity
* mixed ancestry, species, and labor roles may then produce unstable loyalty bands, covert resistance, survival collaboration, and uneven trust under external rule instead of one clean faction alignment

This keeps externally pressured settlement economy and layered local loyalty inside the polity-driven settlement boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold dispersed frontier homesteads, refugee-driven emergence, and seasonal market hardening into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* habitation should support large ranches, family compounds, trapper camps, and other dispersed frontier clusters rather than assuming every meaningful settlement begins as one compact town node
* refugee camps and other shock-driven civilian concentrations may harden into semi-permanent or permanent settlements over time when pressure, safety, and service density persist
* repeated seasonal trade, artisan clustering, or labor surplus may likewise turn temporary exchange points into proto-urban service districts without a top-down founding event

This keeps frontier homestead clustering, refugee settlement emergence, and market-to-village transition inside the settlement-generation boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold edge-terrain fort siting and support-capacity growth into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* forts and frontier sites may be seeded at fords, bluff edges, lake shores, river mouths, marsh rims, passes, islands, forest edges, or other edge conditions so approach vectors and defensive logic emerge from placement as much as from architecture
* repeated trade, defense, or logistics use around those sites should feed later support-capacity growth and attached civilian spillover rather than leaving them as isolated military dots forever

This keeps edge-terrain fort placement and support-capacity emergence inside the settlement-generation boundary.

#### Relations
_No relations_

* support licensed route protection where approved ships are spared while unlicensed traffic becomes eligible prey
* connect captured cargo to controlled resale, auction, or re-entry into local commerce rather than simple destruction only

* connect the layer to existing transport, legitimacy, covert-access, and service-node systems where appropriate

Constraints:

Implement multi-key access control so locks can validate combinations, socket-specific credentials, and persistent failure states instead of simple one-key success.

* enforce exact socket, order, or placement matching where the right key in the wrong hole still fails
* support persistent lock damage, jam, disablement, or reset state when incorrect credentials are used

* deterministic only
* no universal combinatorics sandbox for every lock in the project
* no free infinite retries with no consequence on protected locks
* prefer compact lock-network rules over bespoke per-door scripts
* keep credential use and failure outcomes legible enough for debugging and player explanation

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one lock validates a multi-key combination
* at least one credential must match a specific socket, order, or placement rule
* at least one incorrect insertion causes persistent failure or disablement

### SPE-136 - Persistent hidden search and diminishing retries
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 3
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:45:52.965Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded hidden-search layer so undiscovered content persists across visits, each search attempt costs time, repeated retries lose efficiency, and any hidden item can only be claimed once.

Scope:

* preserve hidden finds, caches, and room-specific secret content until actually discovered rather than regenerating or disappearing on a failed check
* charge explicit time cost for search actions so repeated attempts create real operational pressure
* reduce success quality or odds on repeated equivalent retries instead of allowing infinite equal-quality rerolls
* hard-stop discovery once a hidden object or cache has been claimed so it cannot be found again by the same or later searchers
* support search targeting by room sub-surface or local feature where relevant
* connect the layer to existing packet-navigation, knowledge, weird-space, and action-budget systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no costless infinite search loop
* no hidden-content respawn after recovery
* prefer compact retry-state and claimed-state rules over sprawling per-object search trees
* keep search pressure and persistence legible enough for player explanation

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one hidden find persists after a failed search and can be found later
* each search attempt consumes explicit time in at least one reusable flow
* repeated retries degrade odds or quality deterministically
* once a hidden item is claimed, it cannot be rediscovered again
* targeted tests cover hidden-state persistence, retry degradation, time cost, and one-time claim behavior

_No relations_

### SPE-135 - Structured room-key records
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 3
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:38:30.875Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded room-authoring schema so keyed spaces can separately represent immediate sensory read, floor-state behavior, active hazards, resident entities, and discoverable hidden content instead of flattening all room data into one prose block.

Scope:

* support distinct authoring fields for first-glance sensory presentation, ground or floor state, trap or hazard layer, denizen layer, and hidden or found-content layer
* preserve different runtime expectations for those layers so the same room can contain visible ambience, conditional ground behavior, dormant hazards, living residents, and discoverable hidden content without ambiguity
* support compact room outputs that can be surfaced differently for player-facing presentation, internal simulation, and search resolution
* connect the schema to existing site-space, occupancy, weird-space, and clue systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no need for a giant omnischema covering every possible room genre at once
* no flattening hidden or hazard content into ordinary flavor text only
* prefer a small number of explicit keyed layers over sprawling ad hoc prose conventions
* keep the schema inspectable enough for authoring review and debugging

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one room uses distinct sensory, floor, hazard, denizen, and hidden-content layers
* at least two of those layers can change independently without breaking the room packet
* targeted tests cover deterministic room-layer interpretation, search interaction with hidden content, and differentiated runtime use of keyed layers

Reconciliation update

Fold distributed map fragments across keyed rooms into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* rooms may expose partial site maps or closure panels that reveal only one slice of a larger layout
* the keyed schema should preserve those fragments as separate discoverable surfaces whose combined recovery improves whole-site understanding over time

This keeps partial map intelligence as a first-class room-layer output inside the structured room-key boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold room-key support for immediate sensory read, interactables, and latent conflict payloads into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* small locations should be keyed through concise packets that separate first-glance presentation, interactable elements, and likely conflict or hazard surfaces rather than freeform prose only
* this schema should stay light enough to support low-prep scenario work while still preserving meaningful interactive density

This keeps concise room-key structure and reusable interactive payloads inside the structured room-key boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold composite room-state and object-class evidence finds into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* room outcomes may deliberately mix emptiness, treasure, monsters, traps, animals, insects, disrepair, and odd finds in one composite state rather than generating each category in total isolation
* environmental finds should often resolve as object classes with subtype refinement such as tools, armor, containers, books, fittings, furniture, icons, or battle remnants, making evidence-rich clutter and site storytelling as important as pure treasure bundles
* structural decay such as cracked wells, sinkholes, collapsed floors, or compromised ceilings should remain first-class interactive room state rather than passive descriptive color

This keeps composite room-state generation, evidence-heavy object finds, and interactive disrepair inside the structured room-key boundary.

_No relations_

- Updated: 2026-04-25T15:04:25.845Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement flexible aftermath seeding so stable site shells can host movable encounter and aftermath packages without losing structural coherence.

Scope:

* support authored sites with fixed geometry but relocatable actor, aftermath, or encounter packages
* distinguish fixed structural truth from movable occupancy, allegiance, or aftermath state
* support repeatable replays of the same shell with different placements, alignments, or hidden packages while preserving site identity
* preserve inspectable links between placement rules, package slots, and structural constraints
* connect flexible placement to occupancy, packet navigation, and authoring systems

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no unrestricted random room shuffling with no authored logic
* no loss of core site identity through excessive permutation
* prefer compact package slots and placement rules over bespoke per-room rewrite guidance
* keep the fixed-shell versus movable-package relationship legible in authoring review

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one site supports movable actor or encounter packages within a fixed shell
* relocating a package changes aftermath meaningfully without breaking site logic
* fixed room geometry and movable occupancy are represented separately in at least one reusable flow
* targeted tests cover deterministic placement variance, shell stability, and repeatable aftermath setup

#### Relations
_No relations_

### SPE-133 - Sleeper conditioning and triggered obedience
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 3
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:43:49.458Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded control layer so otherwise normal personnel can carry preloaded obedience scripts that activate later from a specific symbol, token, or visual trigger rather than only from live mind-control.

Scope:

* support sleeper conditioning applied ahead of time to ordinary actors
* activate conditioned behavior from a later trigger such as a symbol, badge, disk, phrase, or carried token
* allow conditioned actors to obey a narrow command family without requiring the controller to be continuously present or actively dominating the mind
* keep trigger recognition, obedience scope, and deactivation bounded so not every command becomes universally valid
* connect the layer to existing hidden-state, legitimacy, and faction systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no universal remote puppet system
* no flattening conditioned actors into permanent mindless minions
* prefer compact script types and trigger rules over sprawling bespoke hypnosis trees
* keep cause, trigger, and obedience scope legible enough for later discovery and counterplay

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one ordinary actor can be preconditioned and later activated by a specific symbol or token
* obedience scope is narrower than full permanent mind takeover in at least one reusable flow
* at least one counterplay path exists through trigger denial, removal, or discovery
* targeted tests cover deterministic preloading, trigger activation, bounded obedience, and counterplay

_No relations_

### SPE-132 - Transformation control with upkeep limits
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T00:46:28.758Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement managed transformation control so self-transformation and proxy transformation operate under explicit upkeep, focus, and reversion rules.

Scope:

* support controllers who sustain both their own altered form and one or more lesser transformed proxies under explicit upkeep ceilings
* enforce attention, upkeep, or capacity limits so some combinations cannot be sustained simultaneously
* revert subordinate forms when the controller is unconscious, disrupted, overloaded, or reallocates capacity elsewhere
* support focus objects that stabilize or enhance transformation and meaningfully degrade outcomes when lost or destroyed
* support partial manifestation such as selective weapons, markers, senses, or morphology without full-form rewrite
* support explicit transformation-retention rules so permissions, cognition, defenses, or tool access do not survive every form change equally

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no unrestricted polymorph sandbox with infinite simultaneous upkeep
* no full concentration simulator for every illusion or disguise
* prefer compact upkeep ceilings, focus states, and reversion rules over bespoke per-creature exceptions
* keep controller burden and reversion causes inspectable enough for debugging and explanation

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one controller sustains both self and proxy transformation under explicit limits
* at least one subordinate form reverts when upkeep breaks
* at least one focus object materially changes transformation stability or capability
* at least one partial manifestation path exists without requiring full-form transformation
* targeted tests cover deterministic upkeep limits, focus loss, controller disruption, and proxy reversion

#### Relations
_No relations_

### SPE-131 - Concurrent multi-team site state
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T10:27:30.218Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement concurrent site state so one location can host multiple active teams with overlapping but non-identical objectives, alliances, and knowledge states.

Scope:

* support several active teams or factions operating concurrently inside one site rather than one primary hostile force only
* let each team hold its own objectives, hostility posture, local knowledge, response timing, and activation state
* support shifting pairwise alignments where some groups bargain, exploit, or attack opportunistically without becoming permanent allies
* preserve recoverable state for unconscious, hidden, delayed, or arriving teams so the site can branch based on who is found, revived, triggered, or ignored
* support compact group packets instead of simulating every actor individually where the group view is clearer
* connect the layer to occupancy, hidden-state, alert, and regional-state systems

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no unrestricted free-for-all political simulator
* no need to simulate every actor individually when group packets are clearer
* prefer compact team-state packets and relationship rules over sprawling faction scripts
* keep concurrent agendas legible enough that players can reason about them

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one site supports three or more active teams with distinct objectives
* team hostility or cooperation can differ pairwise rather than using one universal site alignment
* unconscious or delayed participants can still matter later through recovery, discovery, or activation
* targeted tests cover deterministic multi-team coexistence, branching response, and state persistence across concurrent agendas

_No relations_

### SPE-130 - Multi-axis fatigue, stress, and exhaustion
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-23T03:39:50.642Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement distinct fatigue and stress channels so physical wear, mental strain, combat fatigue, and overdrive debt accumulate differently instead of collapsing into one generic depletion meter.

Scope:

* support at least three bounded channels such as physical exhaustion, mental exhaustion, and combat or crisis stress
* support different causes and recovery paths so travel, fear, overuse, overtesting, prolonged engagement, and concentration pressure do not all land on one identical track
* support thresholds that degrade readiness, morale, concentration, perception, or combat performance differently depending on which channel is elevated
* support repeated capability-use strain and multi-stat overdrive states with explicit recovery debt or lockout
* connect acute combat pressure to longer-horizon degradation without turning every short conflict into permanent damage
* connect the layer to recovery, medical, action-budget, confrontation, and knowledge systems

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no sprawling needs simulator with dozens of meters
* no flattening all fatigue into one generic penalty ladder
* prefer a small number of explicit channels and bounded cross-effects over many bespoke status families
* keep channel causes and recovery rules legible enough for player explanation

Acceptance criteria:

* at least three distinct fatigue or stress channels exist with different causes or consequences
* at least two downstream systems react differently depending on which channel is elevated
* at least one recovery path helps one channel more than another
* at least one repeated-use or overdrive path imposes explicit later strain or lockout
* targeted tests cover deterministic accumulation, differentiated recovery, and cross-effects

_No relations_

### SPE-129 - Identity-Overwrite Pools & Possession Traps
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Duplicate
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-22T09:47:19.366Z

#### Description
Goal:
Implement a bounded identity-trap layer so pools, mirrors, vats, and similar environments can overwrite appearance, trap consciousness, or enable possession with blended memory state instead of resolving as ordinary damage hazards.

Scope:

* support layered identity effects that begin with cosmetic overwrite or idealized reflection and may escalate into possession, blended memory, or body-state replacement
* support subtle transformation states that may be mistaken for stress, confusion, or ordinary strain rather than broadcasting themselves perfectly
* support long-duration consciousness entrapment where a mind remains aware but cannot leave except through bounded transfer or possession mechanics
* support downstream authorization, hostility, and social handling reacting to apparent form when relevant rather than only to underlying hidden identity
* connect the layer to existing hidden-state, knowledge, relic, and anomaly systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no unrestricted body-hopping sandbox
* no instant identity rewrite on every reflective surface
* prefer a small number of explicit trap patterns over sprawling freeform metamorphosis logic
* keep identity truth, apparent form, and possession state inspectable enough for debugging and later consequence handling

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one pool or mirror can overwrite apparent identity without immediately forcing full possession
* at least one path escalates from cosmetic transformation into possession or blended memory state
* at least one consciousness-entrapment case persists until a bounded transfer or possession escape condition is met
* apparent form changes at least one downstream authorization, hostility, or social outcome
* targeted tests cover deterministic overwrite, possession escalation, subtle state surfacing, and long-duration entrapment behavior

#### Relations
- duplicate: SPE-126 Identity-overwrite traps and possession escalation

### SPE-128 - Cursed Life-Anchor Relics
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Duplicate
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-22T10:36:36.775Z

#### Description
Goal:
Implement a bounded relic layer so certain artifacts can anchor, preserve, transfer, or corrupt a life-state at severe long-term cost instead of behaving like ordinary beneficial items.

Scope:

* support relics that preserve or tether an actor's continued existence through an external anchor object
* allow direct removal, transfer, or misuse of the anchor to trigger catastrophic outcomes such as death, insanity, undeath, body corruption, or progression loss
* support later carriers or handlers accumulating curse effects over time rather than suffering only one immediate penalty
* distinguish between preserving life, transferring burden, and corrupting the host so not all life-anchor relics act like simple immortality devices
* connect the layer to existing relic, identity-state, medical, and bound-entity systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no universal resurrection-item sandbox
* no flattening of severe curse states into a short-lived debuff
* prefer a small number of explicit anchor-state patterns over many bespoke phylactery variants
* keep cause and consequence legible enough for long-term state review

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one relic can preserve or tether a life-state externally
* at least one handling path can kill or catastrophically harm the remover or wearer
* at least one later carrier accumulates corruption, drain, or transformation over time
* targeted tests cover anchor persistence, transfer/removal outcomes, and long-horizon curse consequences

#### Relations
- duplicate: SPE-125 Cursed life-anchor relics and transfer fallout

### SPE-127 - Clue artifacts and rumor packets
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 3
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T13:56:32.324Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded clue-artifact layer so maps, journals, notes, sketch plans, and similar in-world objects can guide players through stylized or incomplete representations while unlocking site-specific rumor packets.

Scope:

* support player-facing artifact clues that are distinct from internal truth-state and may be biased, handwritten, incomplete, stylized, or medium-specific
* support paired rumor packets tied to a specific discovered clue or location
* allow rumor entries to mix true, false, inverted, and partially true statements rather than only vague hints
* let rumor quality vary by source distance, local witness access, archives, or specialist knowledge rather than one flat retrieval rule
* preserve author voice, fear, and personal narrative bias in clue artifacts instead of flattening them into neutral map output
* connect the layer to existing knowledge, scouting, packet-navigation, and map systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no freeform procedural prose generation for every clue
* no requirement that every clue resolves to exact coordinates or full truth
* prefer compact artifact templates and rumor-packet rules over bespoke one-off narrative handling
* keep the link between player-facing artifact and internal site truth inspectable enough for authoring review

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one site has a player-facing clue artifact distinct from the internal truth representation
* at least one artifact unlocks a bounded rumor packet tied to that site
* rumor packets support true, false, inverted, or partial entries in one reusable flow
* source distance or specialist access changes rumor quality in at least one deterministic case
* targeted tests cover artifact linkage, dual-representation handling, and rumor retrieval

_No relations_

### SPE-123 - Ecological perimeter IFF and coordinated living defenses

- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-25T20:23:24.507Z

Goal:  
Implement ecological perimeter defense so living barriers, token-mediated IFF, trained fauna, and territorial nodes can defend a site as one coordinated mesh.

Scope:

* support token-, symbol-, or profile-mediated friend-or-foe recognition for fauna, monsters, plants, and summoned defenders
* support living barriers that regenerate, resist ordinary breach methods, and defend both surface and subsurface approaches
* support cross-species coordination so aligned defenders can ignore one another, share targets, reinforce nearby nodes, and answer zonewide calls
* support perimeter layouts that extend across surrounding territorial nodes such as burrows, lakes, ruins, rooftops, and approach routes
* support sacred-site screening, perimeter ecology, and species-specific wayfinding substrates where local infrastructure itself helps authorized movement and blocks intruders
* connect the layer to occupancy, regional map, siegeworks, and bound-entity systems

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no full animal-society simulator
* no need to make every plant an active combat actor
* prefer compact IFF rules, living-barrier states, and territorial-node links over sprawling ecology scripts
* keep layered perimeter behavior legible enough for intrusion planning and player explanation

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one perimeter uses token- or signal-mediated IFF to separate authorized from unauthorized entrants
* at least one living barrier self-repairs or resists breach beyond ordinary wall semantics
* at least one cross-species defender network shares targets or reinforcement under explicit rules
* at least one defense extends into surrounding territorial nodes outside the core structure
* at least one authorized controller can call local defenders across a bounded zone
* targeted tests cover deterministic IFF, barrier recovery, defender coordination, territorial-node defense, and zonewide call behavior

### SPE-122 - Portal classes and endpoint rules
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-27T09:53:52.351Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement portal infrastructure so gates differ by trust class, endpoint behavior, observability, and failure risk instead of acting as one always-safe network.

Scope:

* support multiple portal classes with different safety, control, and reliability profiles
* treat endpoints as configurable objects with throughput limits, size classes, cadence or recharge rules, and receiver-state fallback behavior
* support reciprocal, one-way, random, partial-function, and visible-but-dead route semantics under one typed model
* support partial route-state indicators, layered transit-reveal tools, attribution controls, and route reversal under bounded conditions
* support contamination, stowaway, or piggyback transit failures where unauthorized riders can cross with legitimate traffic
* support short-range keyed transit, token-bound passage, temporary openings, and moving-entity-generated portal creation where relevant

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no freeform gate scripting for every endpoint
* no universal teleport simplification
* prefer compact portal classes and endpoint properties over sprawling per-gate exception trees
* keep class differences and route observability legible enough for players to learn which networks are safe or risky

Acceptance criteria:

* at least two portal classes behave differently in safety or control
* at least one endpoint rule such as throughput, size class, recharge, or receiver fallback materially changes transport outcome
* at least one route indicator reveals partial state without fully identifying the traveler
* at least one bounded control action can attribute or reverse a route
* at least one contamination or piggyback path exists as a deterministic rare failure state
* targeted tests cover class differences, endpoint rules, partial observability, control actions, and contamination behavior

#### Relations
- related: SPE-765 Anomalous route graphs and misrouting
- related: SPE-117 Regional-to-site map stack

Implement biome content tables so terrain regions emit ordinary flora and fauna while rare flora can become authored anomaly nodes with distinct interactions.

* at least one rare flora result becomes a discrete anomaly node rather than ordinary dressing

Goal:  
Implement the nested map stack so one location can resolve coherently across regional, approach, and site scales instead of existing as disconnected map artifacts.

* preserve named features such as roads, ferries, rivers, settlements, keeps, lairs, and civic anchors across scale transitions
* support detached submaps, cutaways, sectional routing views, and campus-style compounds where one site needs more than one plan view
* support partial reveal independently by scale without losing the shared underlying location identity

* connect scale transitions to existing regional operations, site-space, navigation, and packet-state systems

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no universal free-zoom world simulator
* no need to render every scale at once
* prefer compact scale-link rules and shared location identity over bespoke one-off transition code
* keep scale changes legible enough that players understand how regional points become approach spaces and then sites

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one location is represented coherently across regional, local, and site scales
* moving between scales preserves named features and route continuity in at least one reusable flow
* partial knowledge can differ by scale without losing shared location identity
* at least one site uses more than one linked view such as section, cutaway, or detached submap

### SPE-116 - Scenario pressure moves with agency guardrails
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-23T03:35:59.218Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement structured scenario pressure moves so optional or director-style modes can inject clocks, threats, and changing circumstances without directly controlling player characters.

Scope:

* define a compact taxonomy of pressure moves such as immediate moves, reveal moves, introduction moves, and clock-starting moves
* allow these moves to add threats, reveal new actors, start timed pressures, alter zone conditions, or inject bounded scenario complications
* support persistent or evade-only hazards with explicit origin, duration, and persistence rules when introduced by a move
* enforce a hard agency guardrail where pressure moves constrain circumstances around players but never directly choose their actions
* support objective-motivation alignment so pressure escalates what players already care about rather than advancing only through coercion
* connect moves to optional scenario modes, pacing state, occupancy, hidden-target tracking, and shared scenario registry systems

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no unrestricted GM-fiat engine
* no direct player-character mind control through director moves
* prefer a small set of explicit move families over bespoke event scripting
* keep introduced pressure legible enough that later authority handoff can preserve it cleanly

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one scenario mode can apply structured pressure moves across repeated time segments
* at least one move starts a clock or persistent pressure state rather than resolving instantly
* at least one move introduces an evade-only or non-defeatable hazard with explicit persistence rules
* agency guardrails prevent direct player-character control while still allowing strong environmental pressure
* targeted tests cover deterministic move legality, application, persistence, and agency enforcement

### SPE-115 - Peril escalation and survival-gate resolution
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-23T03:35:59.291Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement the peril layer so dangerous failures escalate survival pressure over time instead of resolving only as harmless failure or immediate removal.

Scope:

* define a peril or equivalent danger meter that rises through dangerous failure, severe losses, or repeated exposure
* trigger survival gates after qualifying failures, with worsening odds as peril increases
* support severe-loss escalation that worsens the survival channel beyond ordinary peril gain
* distinguish low-risk failures that can escalate into danger from already perilous attempts that resolve immediate fallout
* connect peril to confrontation, action-risk tags, dramatic survival resources, and attrition systems
* keep the reason for each survival check explicit enough for player-facing explanation

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no generic hit-point replacement across the whole project
* no constant death-save spam on every small setback
* prefer a compact meter plus explicit survival gates over sprawling injury trees
* keep causes and thresholds legible to the player

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one dangerous failure adds peril and triggers a survival check
* survival odds worsen as tracked peril rises
* at least one severe-loss path worsens the survival channel beyond normal peril increase
* low-risk failures can escalate into dangerous retries in at least one reusable flow
* targeted tests cover deterministic peril gain, survival-gate triggering, worsening odds, and severe-loss escalation

- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:27:41.921Z

Implement the pre-mission query budget so briefing intelligence comes through a limited number of narrow questions instead of an exposition dump or one generic scouting roll.

Scope:

* support operation packets that publish a finite query budget before launch
* support authored question sets that reveal narrow answers about route truth, target state, local threats, environmental hazards, or objective context
* preserve uncertainty by limiting the budget and leaving unanswered channels unknown at mission start
* support graded answers such as partial, low-resolution, witness-limited, or false-confidence outputs without collapsing into arbitrary misinformation
* connect pre-mission questions to existing intel, briefing, packet-navigation, and scenario-authoring systems
* expose what was asked, what remains unknown, and what confidence level was gained

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no freeform ask-anything dialogue system
* no full pre-mission planning minigame
* prefer compact authored question sets and answer grades over broad natural-language interrogation
* keep the surface legible enough that players understand what information they chose to buy or leave unknown

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one mission exposes a finite set of authored pre-mission questions before deployment
* spending the query budget changes starting knowledge materially in at least one reusable flow
* at least one answer is narrow or partial rather than full route truth
* unanswered questions leave a real unknown affecting risk or routing
* targeted tests cover deterministic query availability, answer resolution, budget depletion, and mission-start knowledge state

* at least one generated understructure includes an entrance state that changes access method or required equipment
* at least one origin class produces a distinct topology pattern relative to another class

### SPE-106 - Aggregate Battle Units & Routed Morale States
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: In Review
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:56:12.132Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded large-scale battle layer so aggregated forces can fight through phase-ordered engagement, morale collapse, facing pressure, and table-driven outcome bands without requiring one-actor-per-combatant simulation.

Scope:

* support aggregate units whose represented headcount varies by actor class, mount class, heavy asset class, or special creature family rather than one universal compression ratio
* support an ordered battle loop with explicit movement, missile, melee, morale, and rally windows, including mutual-resolution segments where appropriate
* support facing-dependent threat projection, zone-of-control style movement denial, and restrictions on chaining movement through hostile influence zones
* support weighted stacking or occupancy capacity rather than one-space-per-unit only
* support localized combined fire, hard per-segment firing or casting cadence limits, and temporary lockouts between melee and ranged contribution where relevant
* support melee eligibility and attack resolution through bounded factor-versus-defense tables or equivalent authored outcome matrices rather than continuous damage formulas alone
* support morale failure branches such as hold, retreat, disorder, and routed states, with routed forces entering persistent altered mobility and vulnerability behavior until recovered or eliminated
* support special-unit wound accumulation for large or rare assets, plus command-loss berserk or indiscriminate behavior where supervisory control matters
* connect the layer to existing confrontation, regional-state, reserve, and command-coordination systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no full miniature-wargame simulation for every mission
* no one-actor tactical accounting at army scale
* prefer compact aggregate packets, phase windows, and result tables over sprawling unit-by-unit micromanagement
* keep battle outcomes legible enough for campaign follow-through and post-action explanation

Acceptance criteria:

* at least two distinct unit families use different aggregation scales in one reusable battle flow
* at least one battle uses an ordered multi-phase loop with a mutual-resolution segment
* facing or control zones constrain at least one movement path materially
* morale can produce more than binary pass/flee, including at least one persistent routed-state behavior
* at least one special unit survives multiple hits through a separate accumulation model
* targeted tests cover deterministic aggregation, phase order, control-zone movement denial, morale branching, and special-unit durability

Reconciliation update

Fold horde templates, commander-linked group behavior, and collective action packets into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* large groups should be representable as aggregate entities with their own action set such as surround, protect, stampede, or equivalent collective behaviors instead of one-actor-per-body accounting
* commander-linked variants may alter morale, movement, or available collective actions without forcing the horde to dissolve into discrete actors

This keeps horde abstraction and commander-linked collective behavior inside the aggregate battle-unit boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold area-line battle geometry, commander-presence optimization, and hidden pre-battle deployment into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* battles may resolve through connected line, flank, reserve, and support areas rather than free placement, with engagement locks and eligibility varying by unit class and current area state
* some formations should gain attack, defense, or morale advantages only while a specific commander is physically attached to the engagement area rather than issuing orders remotely
* certain deployment effects may place units in fully hidden battlefield positions before contact, leaving them untargetable and absent from ordinary threat accounting until reveal conditions are met

This keeps area-based battle geometry, present-commander force bonuses, and pre-reveal deployment states inside the aggregate battle-unit boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold apex-commander battlefield overlays into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some singular high-tier entities should attach to an aggregate force as commander overlays that modify morale certainty, melee pressure, defense, spell resistance, or similar army-wide outputs without dissolving into ordinary unit accounting
* these overlays should remain interoperable with personal-scale dossiers so one named anomaly can matter both as an individual actor and as a battle-scale modifier packet

This keeps boss-as-battlefield-modifier logic and cross-scale commander overlays inside the aggregate battle-unit boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold camp-wide haunt disruption and formation-scale supernatural pressure into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* spectral, undead, or haunt-class phenomena should be able to disrupt camps, formations, or army support posture at a larger scale than one adventuring party, altering morale, rest quality, cohesion, or readiness across grouped forces
* these effects should remain distinct from ordinary battlefield spell packets by behaving as persistent supernatural pressure on encampments, columns, or grouped troops rather than one instant blast

This keeps formation-scale haunting and camp-level supernatural disruption inside the aggregate battle-unit boundary.

_No relations_

* no per-weapon bespoke minigame

* at least one restraint tool imposes partial limb disablement with secondary defensive effects

### SPE-102 - Alert networks and response choreography
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 3
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-27T05:53:16.776Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded site-alert layer so warnings propagate through linked devices, messengers, and prepared response plans with staged escalation instead of collapsing directly from stealth to universal site awareness.

Scope:

* support alarm devices and channels such as horns, gongs, trophies, sirens, runners, shouted alerts, or other local warning mechanisms with explicit activation requirements and delays
* define how alert signals propagate across a site through audibility, line routing, messenger travel, or linked node relationships rather than by omniscient instant broadcast
* support downstream responder choreography so nearby defenders can reorient into ambush, reinforce chokepoints, arm heavy positions, wake reserves, or fall back to prepared lines when specific alerts succeed
* keep alert state granular enough to distinguish local awareness, partial site alert, and full mobilization
* allow interruption, delay, destruction, or failure of the alert path where intruders act before the network completes activation
* connect the layer to existing occupancy, time-pressure, site-space, and command-coordination systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no full stealth-sim acoustics model
* no binary hidden/fully-alert site logic as the only path
* prefer compact alert stages, signal channels, and response packets over bespoke per-room scripting
* keep activation and propagation legible enough for debugging and authoring review

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one site uses more than one alert channel or device family
* at least one alert requires travel time, activation time, or both before downstream responders change state
* at least one response path repositions defenders or prepares an ambush instead of simply spawning awareness
* intruders can prevent, delay, or localize at least one alert chain through bounded action
* targeted tests cover deterministic alert activation, propagation delay, staged awareness, and responder-state changes

Reconciliation update

Fold constant patrol circuits and disturbance-threshold release behavior into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* guards may move on authored repeating circuits with predictable recurrence windows rather than remaining static room defenders
* sufficiently loud force, noise, or central disturbance may advance alert state far enough to release contained monsters or mobilize remote defenders beyond the current room

This keeps route-based patrol timing and threshold-driven wider release logic inside the alert-network boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold hidden alarm lattices and anti-scout trap doctrine into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some perimeters should combine tripwires, subtle watcher positions, concealed signal paths, and nested belts of discouraging traps that most infiltrators may not even realize they triggered at first
* these layers may be tuned to wound, slow, mark, or frighten scouts rather than to annihilate armies, preserving mission-shaped defensive doctrine inside the alert network

This keeps subtle perimeter alarm lattices and anti-infiltrator trap intent inside the alert-and-response boundary.

### SPE-101 - Linked Traversal Failures & Tether Propagation

- Status: Backlog

- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop

Implement a bounded linked-traversal hazard layer so ropes, tethers, formation links, and shared precarious footing can propagate failure across multiple actors instead of resolving as isolated slip checks.

Scope:

* support hazardous ledges, narrow crossings, exposed climbs, and similar traversal segments where slip or misstep can escalate into edge loss or fall state
* allow actors to be physically linked by rope, tether, stretcher, extraction line, or similar connection with both benefits and shared-risk consequences
* propagate failure through linked actors under explicit conditions so one slip can pull a second actor, partial group loss can escalate to whole-team failure, or successful bracing can arrest the cascade
* support bounded environmental modifiers such as ice, wind, poor visibility, burden, or unstable footing without requiring a full movement simulator
* distinguish partial fall outcomes, full drop outcomes, and cushioned or terrain-modified landing states where relevant
* connect the layer to existing spatial, recovery, pursuit, and action-budget systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no continuous physics simulation
* no universal rope-management minigame
* prefer compact linkage states and cascade thresholds over bespoke per-map scripts
* keep outcomes explainable enough that the player can understand why one slip stayed local while another took the whole linked group over

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one traversal segment can propagate failure through a linked group rather than only the initial actor
* at least one tether both reduces separation risk and increases cascade risk under the wrong conditions
* partial versus whole-group fall outcomes are mechanically distinct in at least one reusable flow
* terrain or landing context changes at least one downstream injury, recovery, or survival result
* targeted tests cover deterministic slip checks, tether propagation, arrest conditions, cascade thresholds, and landing-state consequences

Goal:  
Implement bounded recovery-node and degradation rules so rest, safety, fatigue, and sustenance change operator effectiveness through valid locations and long-horizon maintenance constraints.

Scope:

* support tagged safe or sanctuary nodes that grant recovery actions unavailable in hostile space
* degrade multiple systems under inadequate rest, including competence, resilience, and collapse risk where relevant
* support long-horizon sustenance pressure causing staged decline before terminal failure
* distinguish unsafe pause, ordinary rest, and full sanctuary recovery as materially different paths
* support mission-duration body-state or curse persistence that outlives gear swaps until explicit recovery conditions are met

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no survival-micromanagement grind
* no universal bedroll solution that erases site pressure everywhere
* prefer a compact set of degradation ladders and recovery-node tags over sprawling needs simulation
* keep the system explainable and scenario-sensitive

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one tagged node grants valid protected recovery while ordinary nodes do not
* inadequate rest degrades more than one downstream system deterministically
* sustenance loss causes staged decline rather than instant failure only
* at least one scenario distinguishes unsafe pause, ordinary rest, and full sanctuary recovery
* targeted tests cover deterministic recovery-node validation, fatigue degradation, sustenance decline, and recovery-path differences

_No relations_

### SPE-98 - Runtime Inventory, Tool State, & Contextual Item Use
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, core-loop, system
- Updated: 2026-04-23T05:34:07.818Z

#### Description
Goal:
Implement a bounded runtime item-state layer so carried tools, containers, held media, temporary conjured items, and exact object-target interactions matter operationally instead of collapsing into flat ownership flags.

Scope:

* support physicalized carrying limits using size-sensitive slots, nested containers, and explicit held-versus-stowed state where relevant
* allow some actions or abilities to require an actively held tool, focus object, interface medium, or equipped item at execution time rather than mere ownership
* support object-specific affordance menus such as examine, read, take, apply, repair, or unlock based on current actor, object, and target context
* support exact inventory-object application to environmental targets such as locks, seals, keyed devices, tagged anchors, or prepared loci when those interactions are required
* support temporary instantiated equipment with its own identity, usable properties, and bounded lifetime rather than only buffs on preexisting gear
* support compact equipment failure states such as jam, misfire, degraded readiness, breakage, or tool lockout, and require appropriate capability plus repair tools for restoration where relevant
* connect the runtime layer to existing loadout, structured-definition, action-budget, navigation, and research systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no immersive-sim everything-interacts rewrite
* no full weight-by-gram inventory simulator
* prefer compact carried-state rules, container hierarchies, and context menus over sprawling verb lists
* keep affordance logic inspectable enough for debugging and authoring review

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one actor uses size-sensitive carrying limits with a nested container path
* at least one ability or action is gated by an actively held tool or medium at execution time
* at least one object exposes different valid actions through contextual affordance logic
* at least one environmental problem resolves through exact item-to-target application
* at least one tool can enter a failure state and require both capability and correct repair support to restore
* at least one temporary conjured or instantiated item behaves as a bounded runtime entity rather than only as a stat modifier
* targeted tests cover deterministic carrying state, held-item gating, affordance selection, item-target application, failure/repair flow, and temporary-item lifecycle

_No relations_

* at least one custom-operator bootstrap reaches the same mission loop through the same core interfaces

### SPE-96 - Stateful packet navigation and revisit routing
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 3
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:45:53.144Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded packet-navigation layer so site content can resolve through directed node transitions, revisit-aware state, exact conditional routing, and repeat-run shortcuts without requiring continuous prose scenes or fully bespoke scenario scripting.

Scope:

* support numbered or keyed content packets connected by explicit directed transitions rather than only open-ended scene text
* allow transitions to branch on precise conditions such as role, gear, prior visits, survivor composition, carried items, flags, or stat thresholds
* support solo, duo, and fragmented-party variants of the same content packet without rewriting the whole structure
* make revisits stateful so earlier choices, consumed items, triggered hazards, moved objects, rescued entities, and altered room states can change later returns instead of resetting to default
* support prior-knowledge and repeat-run shortcut flags so known packet chains can compress on later expeditions without breaking progression logic
* allow survivor-composition-dependent rewards, outcomes, and endings where one survivor, both survivors, or no rescue materially changes packet resolution
* support packet-local pacing valves such as time-costed wandering-threat checks, forced retreat endings, recurring doom milestones, or delayed rescue failures that mutate later content
* integrate with existing weird-room logic, knowledge-state, hidden-state, reward provenance, and optional scenario modes where relevant

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no full visual novel scripting engine
* no unrestricted freeform branching graph authoring
* prefer compact packet IDs, condition evaluators, and state flags over sprawling bespoke narrative trees
* keep local rule overrides and packet transitions inspectable enough for debugging and authoring review

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one site can be authored as a directed packet graph with nontrivial conditional routing
* at least one revisit changes outcome based on prior state rather than resetting identically
* at least one repeat-run shortcut or prior-knowledge compression path exists
* at least one packet outcome depends on survivor composition, carried state, or exact prior acquisition
* targeted tests cover deterministic transition routing, revisit persistence, shortcut flags, and packet-local pacing triggers

Goal:  
Implement a bounded apex-entity ecology so top-tier entities are defined by domain authority, observed-behavior intervention, layered hierarchy, and geography-bound presence rather than only by combat statistics.

Scope:

* author apex entities primarily around domain authority, jurisdictional verbs, protected domains, and exceptional presence rather than only offensive numbers
* support conditional intervention triggered by observed deeds, with bounded reward, punishment, favor, or retaliation hooks keyed to doctrine-relative values
* support layered hierarchy for apex families, including rulers, sub-rulers, hero-tier agents, servants, and subordinate packs with different expectations and authority bands
* support geography-bound apex ecology so some entities, servants, and category exemplars are tied to specific regions, layers, sacred sites, or ecological families
* support passive presence effects such as fear, paralysis, awe, or obedience pressure that scale by target tier
* keep apex content as a ceiling reference for campaign balance so such entities remain rare, exceptional, and clearly above ordinary operators

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no universal use of apex entities across every campaign
* no flattening apex entities into generic bosses with different numbers only
* prefer compact doctrine, hierarchy, and intervention rules over sprawling divine-sim detail
* reuse existing structured definitions, legitimacy, hidden-state geography, and reward systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one apex family is authored primarily through domain authority and protected-domain logic
* at least one intervention trigger can reward or punish based on observed conduct in a doctrine-relative way
* at least one apex hierarchy includes differentiated layers such as ruler, intermediary, and servant or subordinate packs
* at least one passive apex-presence effect scales by target tier
* targeted tests or scenario examples cover deterministic intervention, hierarchy behavior, and geography-bound apex presence

### SPE-80 - Autonomous bound entities and risky procedures
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 3
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:38:30.801Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded framework for dangerous bound entities so powerful externalized assets are created, contacted, summoned, controlled, punished, and dismissed through explicit procedures while retaining autonomous wants, maintenance demands, and loophole-prone obedience.

Scope:

* define a compact entity schema that combines type, visible signature, operational abilities, recurring Need, autonomous Desire, and control relationship
* support recurring maintenance demands that create pressure if unmet, plus autonomous agendas that are not reducible to simple orders
* support explicit high-risk procedures for locating or contacting, bringing through, binding or securing, punishing or coercing, and dismissing or banishing these entities
* support loophole-prone control arrangements where wording, procedure, or incomplete binding can produce bounded obedience failure
* support group procedures where multiple operators contribute to one major contact or binding action when relevant
* support indirect-power role lanes that act primarily through bound entities or dangerous external systems rather than innate direct powers

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no unrestricted summoning sandbox
* no treating dangerous companions as passive gear
* prefer a small number of explicit procedures and behavioral drivers over broad freeform occult simulation
* reuse existing support-asset, structured-definition, hidden-state, and action-budget systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one bound entity family uses Need and Desire as active operational pressures
* at least three distinct interaction procedures exist for contacting, summoning, binding, punishing, or dismissing such entities
* at least one bound entity can exploit wording or procedural gaps to act partially outside intended orders
* at least one role or scenario path derives most of its major leverage through bound assets rather than innate direct action
* targeted tests or scenarios cover deterministic entity upkeep, autonomy, and procedure outcomes

Reconciliation update

Fold risky override tools and invocation attention into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some dangerous entities may have rare temporary override tools, seals, or keyed control artifacts that grant short-lived authority at severe retaliation or handling risk rather than safe permanent command
* entities may react to true identifiers, invocation patterns, prohibited research, or improper naming as active attention triggers rather than passive lore only
* these mechanics should stay bounded, explainable, and tightly coupled to control relationships rather than opening a broad occult sandbox

This keeps risky control artifacts, true-identifier leverage, and knowledge-triggered entity attention inside the bound-entity framework.

### SPE-79 - Integrity drift, corruption, and agency-loss thresholds
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-22T13:24:21.999Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement the integrity layer so dangerous methods can corrupt actors over time and catastrophic integrity failure can change control or status.

Scope:

* define a bounded central integrity metric whose framing may vary by campaign, such as conscience, legitimacy, sanity, civic integrity, or inner stability
* allow dangerous methods to impose integrity loss by use alone rather than only by bad downstream outcomes
* support divergence between integrity and other capability growth rather than permanently tying moral or psychological state to competence
* support recovery through explicit renunciation, relinquishment, reparative action, or similarly costly correction rather than passive rebound alone
* support catastrophic thresholds that can transfer an actor into hostile, broken, or no-longer-player-controlled status
* allow some advancement paths to depend on retained integrity rather than repetition alone

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no universal morality simulator by default
* no cosmetic integrity score
* prefer compact thresholds, triggers, and recovery paths over sprawling virtue ledgers
* reuse existing legitimacy, progression, condition, and recovery systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one campaign framing uses a central integrity metric as active gameplay state
* at least one dangerous method causes integrity loss on use alone
* at least one recovery path requires explicit renunciation or reparative action
* a catastrophic threshold changes actor status or player control deterministically
* targeted tests cover integrity drift, recovery, and agency-loss behavior

* adjacent assault support and adjacent defensive contribution both appear in one reusable flow

* at least one force can enter a reduced state rather than being removed immediately
* targeted tests cover deterministic force aggregation, support contribution, result-band selection, and exploitation behavior

### SPE-72 - Supply Network, Transport, & Strategic Node Layer
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-27T08:17:41.706Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded supply-state layer so operational effectiveness depends on traced support lines, vulnerable transport capacity, and control of strategic support nodes rather than only current strength.

Scope:

* represent supply as a first-class campaign state with explicit supported and unsupported statuses rather than only ambient logistics pressure
* derive supply from route-connected sources, trace rules, blocked paths, and strategic support nodes such as depots, command centers, ports, corridors, or equivalent operational hubs
* model transport as separate noncombat mobility assets with explicit lift value, fragility, and supply relevance rather than folding all movement into combat units
* apply deterministic unsupported penalties to movement, action capability, reinforcement entry, or engagement effectiveness where relevant
* connect support tracing to campaign geography, corridor movement, reserve timing, occupation burden, and delayed expansion or establishment where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no full logistics wargame rewrite
* no hidden support calculation with no player-facing explanation
* prefer a small number of explicit support states, source types, and trace rules over sprawling stockpile simulation
* reuse existing map, route, reserve, and support-asset systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one operation or campaign flow derives supply from an actual connected source rather than only local presence
* unsupported state changes at least two downstream behaviors such as movement, effectiveness, or reinforcement timing
* at least one transport asset class exists as a distinct vulnerable logistics piece
* at least one strategic node matters for support, control, or scoring beyond direct combat value
* targeted tests cover deterministic supply tracing, blocked support paths, unsupported penalties, and transport-node interactions

Reconciliation update

Fold rapid long-range transit doctrine into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* world-scale movement may mix dense local tactical sites with fast long-range node-to-node travel when transport capability, access permissions, and route integrity allow it
* long-range transit should stay operationally meaningful through interception risk, node control, and transport fragility rather than becoming free teleportation

This keeps fast long-range travel logic inside the transport and strategic-node boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold capital-port primacy into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some states should treat ports, harbors, and capital cities as the true logistics and control surfaces, with inland precision mattering less than whether sea access, toll points, and merchant throughput remain intact
* trade-route interference should therefore be a first-class escalation trigger because disrupting fleets, ports, ferries, or river toll arteries can strike the state more directly than vague border pressure

This keeps city-port-centered logistics and route disruption as conflict trigger inside the transport and strategic-node boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold sea-first route hierarchy and goods-in-kind border economies into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some coastal polities should prefer sea and river movement so strongly that overland travel becomes exceptional, slower, riskier, or culturally secondary even when roads exist on the map
* frontier and mixed-economy regions may also rely heavily on tariffs, barter, salvage, and goods-in-kind extraction, so support and throughput should not assume full coin normalization before logistics become meaningful

This keeps mode-preference logistics and noncash-heavy support flows inside the transport and strategic-node boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold staged customs intake and degraded-port handling into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* ports may route cargo through separate docking, unloading, inspection, and inland transfer stages rather than treating arrival as one instant trade event
* damaged or partly blocked harbors should remain operational under bottlenecked fallback handling instead of flipping directly from open to unusable

This keeps customs-stage logistics and degraded-port throughput inside the transport and strategic-node boundary.

#### Relations
_No relations_

---

### SPE-70 - Hidden-State, Displacement, & Counter-Detection Layer
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:31:58.495Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded hidden-state layer so invisibility, false position, disguise, out-of-phase presence, and similar concealment modes behave as distinct operational states with explicit counters rather than one generic hidden flag.

Scope:

* define multiple hidden-state modalities such as concealed presence, displaced position, disguised identity, signature masking, and out-of-phase or partially absent state where relevant
* support counter-detection and reveal logic that varies by hidden-state mode instead of collapsing all concealment into one detection check
* let hidden-state modes affect targeting, scouting confidence, route safety, response timing, and engagement legality in different ways
* support bounded false-positive and false-position outputs so player-facing projections can diverge from truth-state in legible ways
* connect the layer to knowledge-state, scouting, site-space, structured definitions, and support-asset systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no broad simulation-wide invisibility rewrite
* no one-flag hidden-state model for all cases
* prefer a small number of explicit hidden-state families with clear counters over many bespoke stealth exceptions
* reuse existing reporting and projection helpers where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least three hidden-state modalities are mechanically distinct in one reusable flow
* at least one countermeasure reveals or invalidates one hidden-state mode without automatically solving all others
* player-facing outputs can mislocate, partially reveal, or fail to confirm a hidden target in a deterministic and explainable way
* hidden-state differences affect at least one downstream operational choice such as route selection, response timing, or targeting
* targeted tests cover deterministic hidden-state behavior, counter-detection, and projection mismatch handling

Reconciliation update

Fold glamour, substitution, and altered-return states into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some hidden-state families may operate through glamour-like perception overlays that beautify danger, conceal decay, misframe intent, or replace an ugly truth with a false-safe presentation without changing the underlying state itself
* substitution threats should support missing-person replacement, glamour doubles, altered returnees, and other false-identity cases where the visible person is not what the community assumes
* post-contact returnees may remain subtly changed without being full replacements, carrying durable but partly hidden shifts in affect, fit, luck, speech, or allegiance that informed observers may detect before the wider public does
* invitation-only safety or invitation-only vulnerability may function as threshold-sensitive hidden-state behavior for specific actor families

This keeps glamour overlays, substitution families, altered returnees, and invitation-sensitive hidden identity behavior inside the hidden-state boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold self-remodeling disguise fidelity and pre-reveal battlefield concealment into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some actors may enter a temporary self-disguise state that changes build, face, and social presentation without altering their underlying runtime identity
* disguise quality may depend on whether the imitator has previously observed the target, allowing rough approximation for unknown identities and higher-fidelity impersonation for known ones
* hidden combat deployments may remain untargetable until a reveal condition is met when the concealment mode is full presentation masking rather than mere low visibility, with counter-detection and anti-illusion channels determining whether that immunity collapses early

This keeps temporary self-remodeling disguise, impersonation fidelity, and full pre-reveal masking inside the hidden-state and counter-detection boundary.

_No relations_

- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-24T15:36:12.357Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded covert-operations specialist lane so access-focused operators have distinct verbs, progression, and tradeoffs instead of being flattened into generic utility.

Scope:

* define a specialist lane for covert access tasks such as bypassing locks or security states, removing or disarming traps and response devices, stealth movement, concealment, close-range surprise exploitation, vertical access, and selective filch or recovery actions where relevant
* give the lane explicit progression hooks so covert capability improves through dedicated access, concealment, and precision tasks rather than generic advancement alone
* support task-family resolution with clear success bands, role-sensitive modifiers, and meaningful ceilings so covert operators remain distinct from generalists
* preserve tradeoffs so the lane gains strong access and surprise utility without becoming a dominant all-purpose combat or support track
* connect the lane to routing, site-state, trap, scouting, and knowledge systems where appropriate

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no broad stealth-game rewrite
* no universal bypass of every lockout or site defense
* prefer a compact set of covert verbs and bounded task families over sprawling skill catalogs
* reuse existing role-identity, action-budget, and structured-definition systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one specialist lane has explicit ownership over covert access and security-bypass tasks
* the lane has distinct progression pacing or capability unlocks compared with generalist tracks
* at least two covert task families show readable tradeoffs between specialist and non-specialist operators
* covert success can produce differentiated outcomes such as quiet access, partial compromise, alarmed access, or failed attempt
* targeted tests cover deterministic covert task resolution, progression effects, and non-specialist tradeoffs

Reconciliation update

Fold cover-identity infiltration and covert-role blending into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* hostile or competing agents may enter a site under believable occupational roles such as servant, merchant, scribe, or handmaiden rather than overt military disguise only
* cover identities should allow participation in ordinary social space until a mission surface is triggered, preserving a difference between social access and later revealed intent

This keeps covert-role infiltration and occupational cover identities inside the covert-access boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold hostile recruitment vetting through synthetic field tests into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* criminal, insurgent, or hostile organizations may verify recruits through staged thefts, ambushes, waylaying, or synthetic encounters meant to prove loyalty under apparent risk
* some of those tests may use disguised patrols, fake enemies, or other authored false events rather than genuine uncontrolled danger, with success or hesitation materially changing trust level

This keeps hostile-group vetting and synthetic loyalty-test encounters inside the covert-access boundary.

- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-23T07:29:21.917Z

Implement staged medical response so serious impairments worsen until stabilized and recovery proceeds through typed treatment ladders rather than instant reset.

* represent typed deterioration such as bleeding, contamination, organ compromise, sensory damage, mobility collapse, or related worsening states
* require stabilization before full repair in major cases, with distinct steps for arresting decline, neutralizing harmful agents, restoring function, and long-tail recovery

* support recoverable shock windows immediately after incapacitation before full loss occurs
* support long-horizon regenerative recovery as a distinct restoration path rather than generic fast healing
* expose why an actor must be stabilized now, repaired later, or left with persistent consequences

Constraints:

* no full anatomy simulator
* no instant full reset from major impairment
* prefer a small number of explicit treatment stages and deterioration ladders over sprawling injury trees
* reuse existing condition, downtime, staffing, and recovery systems where possible

* at least one major impairment can worsen over time until the correct stabilization step is applied
* at least two consequence families require materially different treatment paths
* operator quality and available kits change at least one stabilization or treatment outcome deterministically
* a short recoverable shock window exists for at least one incapacitation path
* targeted tests cover deterioration, stabilization sequencing, treatment-quality effects, and downstream recovery impact

Scope:

* represent major asset state through compact subsystem damage such as propulsion, control, payload, structure, crew support, or equivalent components
* support context-sensitive strikes whose effectiveness depends on visibility, target size, movement state, marking quality, route exposure, or delivery conditions
* support disable, capture, occupy, tow, crash, and overcapacity branches as distinct outcomes from total destruction
* support escort, interception, exclusion zones, and cross-domain superiority effects where moving support assets are involved
* support mounted systems, crewed firing positions, scene-device actors, and orbiting or creator-bound support nodes where those materially affect operations
* allow captured or degraded assets to change transport capacity, access, salvage, intelligence, or support transfer downstream

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no full vehicle or fleet simulator
* no detailed per-part engineering model
* prefer compact subsystem states and explicit capture stages over giant stat sheets
* reuse existing action-budget, spatial, and phase-resolution systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one major asset can lose partial functionality through subsystem damage rather than only total loss
* at least one strike outcome changes meaningfully with targeting or environmental conditions
* at least one interaction path distinguishes disable-and-capture from destroy
* at least one degraded or captured asset changes downstream operational capability
* targeted tests cover deterministic subsystem loss, strike-context resolution, and capture-stage outcomes

_No relations_
