### SPE-64 - Cross-Scale Integration & Domain Interface Layer
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-22T09:17:09.594Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded cross-scale integration layer so campaign, squad, and incident resolution share state consistently, while distinct operational domains exchange effects through explicit interfaces instead of hidden coupling.

Scope:

* define how persistent state such as damage, readiness, breakdown, resources, access, and control moves between campaign, squad, and incident-scale systems
* support a minimal core campaign loop with optional modules layered on top through explicit extension points rather than implicit cross-system dependencies
* define stable interfaces between distinct operational domains such as transport/support, regional movement, site operations, and direct incident resolution
* allow domain-specific systems to stay separate internally while still exchanging bounded outputs such as transport availability, strike support, access changes, asset state, and downstream consequences
* keep scale changes and domain handoffs legible enough that the player can understand what carried forward and why

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no universal everything-sim abstraction rewrite
* no hidden cross-scale state duplication
* prefer small explicit handoff contracts over broad shared mutable state
* reuse existing projection, orchestration, and boundary-enforcement work where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one campaign-to-incident and one incident-to-campaign handoff preserve bounded state without contradiction
* at least two distinct operational domains exchange effects through explicit interfaces rather than direct feature coupling
* the base campaign loop remains usable without enabling every optional subsystem
* added modules can attach through bounded integration points without rewriting the core campaign path
* targeted tests cover deterministic cross-scale state carryover, interface contracts, and modular handoff behavior

Reconciliation update

Fold optional high-variance subsystem insertion into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* unusual or destabilizing subsystem families should be supportable as optional inserts with declared state contracts, not as mandatory assumptions of the core campaign shell
* mixed techno-occult, mutant, social-outlier, or table-heavy content families may coexist in one project so long as their integration points, activation assumptions, and downstream effects are explicit

This keeps high-variance modular insertion and mixed-domain optional coexistence inside the cross-scale integration boundary.

### SPE-63 - Fortified Site Breach & Assault Layer
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-23T04:21:25.326Z

#### Description
Goal:
Implement a bounded fortified-site breach layer so defended facilities, blocked access points, and prepared barriers require explicit breach and assault logic instead of resolving as ordinary traversal.

Scope:

* represent walls, sealed access points, barricades, hardened doors, and similar defensive barriers as operational states with explicit breach difficulty and assault consequences
* support bounded breach methods such as forced entry, bypass tools, staged demolition, covert entry, or slow preparatory weakening where relevant
* allow defenders or site state to reinforce, reblock, or exploit breach points in deterministic ways
* connect breach state to exposure, traversal options, interception windows, reinforcement timing, and extraction risk
* keep the layer compact enough to inform assault decisions without becoming a full siege simulator

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no long-form war-game logistics model
* no bespoke one-off breach rules for every site
* prefer compact barrier states, breach methods, and follow-on consequences over detailed engineering simulation
* reuse existing spatial, route, action-budget, and incident systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one fortified site distinguishes ordinary access from breach-required access
* at least two breach approaches produce different time, exposure, or downstream-risk profiles
* at least one breach point can be exploited, defended, or reblocked after initial access
* the player can understand why an assault path was blocked, costly, or newly opened
* targeted tests cover deterministic barrier state, breach progression, breach-point consequences, and downstream operational effects

### SPE-61 - Site Occupancy, Roaming Pressure, & Repopulation Layer
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:34:37.887Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement a bounded site-population layer so location-bound content, roaming pressure, deferred placement, and repopulation are modeled explicitly instead of being collapsed into one generic encounter stream.

Scope:

* distinguish static/site-bound occupancy from mobile/time-driven pressure as separate simulation channels
* support deferred placement for local threats, resources, clues, or complications so content can be instantiated when zones are entered or meaningfully surveyed rather than fully authored up front
* support deterministic repopulation or pressure return where cleared or quiet areas can regain activity based on elapsed time, route exposure, faction movement, anomaly drift, or site state
* connect occupancy and roaming outputs to route safety, scouting freshness, hub opportunities, and incident escalation where relevant
* keep the model compact enough to preserve explainability and avoid a full ecology simulator

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no always-on per-room life sim
* no content respawn for its own sake
* prefer explicit occupancy states, roaming rules, and repopulation triggers over large encounter tables
* reuse existing route, site, knowledge, and opportunity systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one site distinguishes static occupancy from roaming pressure in a way that changes operational outcome
* at least one local content family can be placed lazily on entry, survey, or first meaningful interaction
* at least one cleared or quiet area can regain pressure deterministically from state and elapsed time
* downstream systems can explain whether a threat was site-bound, roaming, newly placed, or repopulated
* targeted tests cover deterministic occupancy placement, roaming movement, repopulation triggers, and explanation output

Reconciliation update

Fold density-guided stocking doctrine into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* site construction may use explicit density targets for how many rooms or major spaces receive meaningful occupancy, treasure, clue, or anomaly content rather than relying only on intuition
* trap density may be governed in relation to populated or notable spaces so hazard cadence stays proportional to stocking density rather than drifting arbitrarily
* low-frequency traversal anomalies or special inserts should respect per-level rarity budgets so special movement features do not overwhelm baseline circulation
* post-layout site finishing should treat occupancy as a distinct stocking pass, where authored anchors are placed first and remaining spaces are filled procedurally afterward
* stocking should remain sparse by default, with null-result encounters and unoccupied spaces preserved deliberately to protect uncertainty and pacing rather than maximizing room-fill rate
* group size should remain a primary encounter-scaling control, and deeper bands should escalate not only by count but by adding specialists, leaders, severe abilities, and mixed-family pressure where appropriate
* macro site intent should constrain local fill so room-level stocking remains subordinate to the overall pressure plan rather than rolling independently in isolation
* large domestic or civic sites may rely on recurring named rosters, room-function haunt logic, emotional-state overlays, and subregion-specific wandering pressure so the whole location feels continuously inhabited by related personalities rather than by disconnected room occupants
* present encounter logic should remain visibly caused by former room function, inherited family structure, and historical tragedy, with local subzones such as galleries, baths, nurseries, courts, gardens, kitchens, or studies producing distinct present-day occupancy behavior
* progression through such sites may escalate through denser recurrence, stranger behavior, and heavier reality distortion rather than only higher raw threat strength
* recurring hostile or unstable personalities should be able to reappear across many rooms through shared surnames, grudges, inherited roles, and local rivalries, making room-to-room continuity part of the ecology rather than isolated flavor text
* mixed fresh-water, salt-water, estuary, and submerged content families should be able to coexist in one bounded region when the terrain itself is transitional rather than cleanly single-biome
* submerged landmarks, caves, ruins, vents, unstable terrain, statues, wrecks, and similar nodes should function as distinct underwater objectives, with environmental pressures such as murk, current, depth, or predators acting as part of the protection model
* nonhuman submerged populations should be treatable as neighboring communities with their own access politics, mistrust, and governance logic rather than only as encounter fauna

This keeps notable-content density targets, trap-density ratios, rarity budgets, two-pass stocking cadence, sparse occupancy doctrine, count-band scaling, compositional depth escalation, macro-plan governed local fill, roster-driven recurrence, history-to-ecology causation, domestic micro-biomes, tone-based escalation, room-to-room roster continuity, transitional mixed-water ecology, submerged objective nodes, environment-protected salvage logic, and neighboring nonhuman enclave ecology inside the occupancy and pacing boundary.

Reconciliation update

Fold fort-maturity controller logic, floor-tier stocking, and independent ecology intrusion into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* principal site controllers should vary by maturity tier so ancient ruins, abandoned forts, active outposts, and strongholds select meaningfully different control actors and entourage expectations
* multi-level forts may stock distinct encounter families, hazards, and finds by basement, ground, and upper bands instead of treating every level as the same ecology
* animals, insects, and other incidental ecologies should remain independent intrusion layers alongside the main controller faction rather than collapsing into one occupant family

This keeps maturity-tier occupancy, floor-banded content, and independent ecological intrusion inside the site-occupancy boundary.

#### Relations
_No relations_

### SPE-60 - Veteran capability drift and age-based role change

- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2

- Updated: 2026-04-22T13:24:21.870Z

Implement long-horizon personnel drift so veteran operators change shape over time instead of remaining physically static forever.

Scope:

* define bounded age- and wear-driven drift for persistent personnel across long campaigns
* support uneven drift where speed, resilience, recovery burden, or acute-response quality decline differently from doctrine fluency, judgment, training value, or institutional knowledge
* connect personnel drift to roster planning, role continuity, specialist retention, replacement pressure, and assignment decisions
* preserve at least one path where veteran value stays operationally meaningful even as some high-strain capability narrows
* surface drift clearly enough in roster views and reports that long-run consequences can be planned around

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no life-sim or dense biography system
* no universal decline curve applied identically to all personnel
* prefer a small number of bounded drift patterns over dense aging simulation
* reuse existing role-identity, attrition, recovery, and progression systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one persistent personnel group changes capability profile over long campaign time
* decline and retained competence are both represented in at least one bounded case
* personnel drift affects at least one planning or assignment decision
* veteran value remains distinct from early-career physical responsiveness in at least one downstream system
* targeted tests cover deterministic drift progression, downstream effects, and explanation output

### SPE-59 - Unknown Interaction Engine
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:31:52.362Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Build the bounded runtime layer for interacting with partially understood systems so anomalies, environments, hazards, routes, devices, and incident surfaces can reveal behavior through use, observation, failure, and containment instead of requiring full upfront understanding.

Scope:

* define interaction patterns where the player can act on a system before its full rules are known
* support deterministic reveal-on-contact, reveal-on-analysis, reveal-on-failure, and reveal-on-containment behavior where unknown systems expose more of themselves through interaction
* support unknown versus partially-known versus confirmed interaction surfaces without bespoke per-feature logic
* support bounded misread, false-confidence, and partial-diagnosis states where early conclusions can be wrong or incomplete
* connect environment-driven behavior so the same system can respond differently under local state, containment state, damage state, or exposure context while remaining explainable after the fact
* support closed-system container logic so unknown behavior can be shaped by local deck/site/zone/container constraints rather than treating every interaction as globally uniform
* connect the engine to canonical tags, conditions, graded outcomes, structured definitions, and template-authored hidden properties where relevant
* keep the system deterministic and reusable across exploration, scouting, incident response, anomaly handling, and site traversal

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no freeform mystery scripting layer
* no giant hidden-rule simulator with no explanation surface
* prefer a small number of reusable interaction patterns over content-specific special cases
* reuse existing reporting, explanation, and projection helpers where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one major interaction path supports acting before full understanding is available
* unknown, partially-known, and confirmed interaction states are mechanically distinct in at least one reusable flow
* at least one reveal path is driven by failure or adverse contact rather than only successful analysis
* environment or containment context changes at least one interaction outcome through the shared engine
* downstream reports or projections can explain what was unknown, what was revealed, and what remains uncertain without raw debug inspection
* targeted tests cover deterministic reveal progression, misread states, context-dependent interaction, and explanation output

Reconciliation update

Fold encounter-state versus true-state classification into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* first-contact records should distinguish surfaced encounter identity from underlying true nature so an observed threat class, rumor label, or field designation can remain provisional until confirmed
* the same entity may present one apparent classification in the field and a different canonical taxonomy entry underneath, with deterministic upgrade paths from suspected to confirmed identity
* reports, projections, and encounter generation should preserve that separation so scenario logic can operate on provisional contact state without pretending full truth is already known

This keeps provisional contact identity and revealed true-state classification inside the unknown-interaction boundary.

_No relations_

* distinguish raw intel, provisional interpretation, confirmed understanding, and reusable operational knowledge

* at least two child consumer systems change behavior based on the shared knowledge model
* provisional, confirmed, and reusable knowledge are mechanically distinguishable in at least one major flow
* the shared model is explicit enough that new acquisition or output channels can consume it without redefining core semantics
* targeted tests cover deterministic knowledge gain, confirmation, operational use, and invalidation behavior at the shared-model layer

- Updated: 2026-04-25T09:39:51.582Z

Implement bounded compliance and breakdown behavior so non-core actors and stressed teams do not obey perfectly under pressure.

Scope:

* tie compliance to leadership presence, ally support, doctrine fit, trust, incentives, perceived danger, treatment history, and current conditions
* support confrontation shock before full engagement where expectation of impact can cause hesitation or collapse
* support collapse propagation where nearby rout or leader loss destabilizes already stressed groups
* preserve player agency by applying this only to bounded actor classes and high-pressure contexts rather than all units at all times

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no full AI behavior simulator
* no blanket removal of player agency across all actor groups
* prefer bounded obedience-state transitions over constant unpredictability
* reuse existing trust, support, leadership, and condition systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one actor category can hesitate, refuse, rout, or break under the right conditions
* compliance changes based on contextual factors rather than static flags alone
* leader loss or nearby collapse can trigger deterministic downstream disruption in at least one case
* the player can inspect or infer the main causes of compliance failure
* targeted tests cover deterministic compliance shifts and resulting operational branches

* at least one dangerous action family can produce self-failure outside its intended effect

### SPE-54 - State-driven action priority in volatile encounters

- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-22T13:24:21.433Z

Goal:  
Implement state-driven action priority for volatile encounters so who acts first depends on current conditions rather than static order alone.

* compute action priority from bounded situational factors such as readiness, posture, exposure, injury, tool state, precision, and aim commitment
* support recalculation as state changes rather than one fixed ordering snapshot
* allow both per-actor and bounded side-phase resolution where the encounter mode calls for it
* distinguish rapid nearest-valid execution from slower explicit target designation when control and time trade off against each other
* expose the main drivers of priority clearly enough that players can understand why a side or actor moved earlier or later

* no universal turn-order system for all gameplay
* prefer bounded sequencing in volatile moments over a constant initiative minigame

* at least one volatile context uses computed action priority rather than static order only
* priority changes when relevant situational factors change
* at least one encounter mode supports bounded side-phase handling without replacing the general model
* the main factors driving action priority are inspectable or inferable to the player
* targeted tests cover deterministic priority calculation and state-driven priority shifts

* districts should develop reusable operational signatures from bundled services, risks, and lead patterns so neighborhood identity is mechanical, not descriptive only
* the hub should continuously emit bounded micro-incidents such as disputes, disappearances, inspections, fires, or public anomalies as part of normal city pressure rather than only through bespoke scenarios

### SPE-52 - Faction Agenda & Internal Cohesion Layer
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-25T01:10:27.869Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Extend faction simulation so factions are not static relationship containers, but internally dynamic institutions with competing agendas, unstable cohesion, and context-dependent reliability.

Scope:

* define internal faction state such as agenda pressure, cohesion, competing priorities, or hidden alignment where appropriate
* support truth distortion, conflicting reports, selective cooperation, or internal betrayal risk when faction state is unstable or divided
* allow faction internal state to affect mission reliability, intel quality, contract follow-through, and access promises
* connect faction internal state to standing, obligations, rumors, and operational outcomes where relevant
* expose bounded signals of instability or conflict without requiring full narrative simulation

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no full political simulator
* no freeform intrigue engine
* prefer bounded internal-state dimensions over large bespoke faction trees
* reuse existing faction, standing, contract, and intel systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one faction has internal state that changes operational reliability or intel quality
* conflicting or distorted information can arise from faction state rather than only raw uncertainty
* the player can inspect bounded signs of faction instability or agenda conflict
* targeted tests cover deterministic faction-state movement, reliability effects, and intel distortion

Reconciliation update

Fold etiquette-driven anomaly polities and proxy conflict into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some anomaly polities may be structured around thematic or seasonal domains whose members share coherent but nonhuman priorities rather than generic malice or simple geography-only borders
* political pressure may operate through etiquette, favor debt, status performance, insult, reciprocity, and symbolic offense rather than direct force alone
* contact outcomes may depend on whether an actor correctly reads rank, role, or protocol and performs toward that status appropriately
* higher-order faction conflict may project into ordinary space through proxies, omens, recruited agents, favors, thefts, marriages, or local interventions rather than open battle only
* some anomaly factions may recruit, abduct, or retain humans for narrow service functions rather than pure destruction, with those roles shaped by the faction’s internal value system

This keeps thematic-domain courts, etiquette pressure, protocol-sensitive status handling, proxy conflict projection, and service-extraction faction behavior inside the faction agenda boundary.

* define canonical legitimacy, authority, sanction, and access state for the agency, delegated operators, and relevant institutions
* gate operations by sanction level, legal cover, status fit, invitation logic, credentials, protocol correctness, and social consent rather than resources alone
* support explicit differences between sanctioned, tolerated, covert, deniable, and unsanctioned action
* support observer-relative, political-state, and jurisdiction-sensitive variation in what is legal, tolerated, or punished
* keep this parent focused on the shared policy model rather than absorbing every local consumer pattern

Child ownership:

* [SPE-418](https://linear.app/spectranoir/issue/SPE-418/actor-mediated-chokepoint-access) owns actor-mediated chokepoint access
* [SPE-517](https://linear.app/spectranoir/issue/SPE-517/intent-and-profile-access-adjudication) owns guardian intent/profile adjudication
* [SPE-406](https://linear.app/spectranoir/issue/SPE-406/access-control-pocket-shelter-and-portal-transit) owns magical access-state grammar
* child issues under this parent should own credential transit, rank-sensitive public access, and jurisdiction admission matrices

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no freeform social simulator
* no sprawling bespoke faction exception layer
* prefer bounded access states and explicit gate logic over one-off narrative permission
* reuse existing standing, routing, service-node, and report systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one operation is gated by legitimacy or sanction rather than resources alone
* sanctioned, covert, and unsanctioned handling produce materially different downstream effects
* at least one access path depends on credentials, protocol correctness, invitation, or social consent
* players can see why an action is blocked, risky, or politically costly before committing
* targeted tests cover deterministic legitimacy changes, access gating, and fallout behavior

#### Relations
- related: SPE-406 Access-state grammar for entry, shelter, and transit

### SPE-49 - Regional Operations Map & Zone Control Layer
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-22T06:14:55.083Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement the regional operations layer so the agency engages with a partially known map of zones, sites, routes, and local pressure, where securing, holding, traversing, tracking, and revisiting territory changes long-run campaign options.

Scope:

* define a regional map or zone structure with partial knowledge, route access, and discoverable site opportunities
* support fog-of-war, hidden branches, route uncertainty, and tracking uncertainty where recon, specialist knowledge, or tools can improve access and confidence
* support terrain, route type, and environmental state as modifiers on movement, tracking, extraction, or operational reach where relevant
* support zone clearing, stabilization, or persistent control so securing an area can reduce local pressure, create support, or unlock follow-up options
* support upkeep and liability for controlled footprint so expansion creates both benefits and maintenance burdens
* support site freshness through deterministic mutation or reseeding so previously known areas can change through instability, rival interference, or new anomaly conditions
* connect regional state to contracts, incidents, standing, staffing burden, and agency infrastructure where relevant

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no free-roam map game rewrite
* no fully simulated empire-management layer
* prefer bounded regional state and route logic over giant geographical simulation
* reuse existing intel, pressure, standing, and infrastructure systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* the campaign includes at least one partially known regional or zone layer that affects available operations
* at least one secured or stabilized zone provides a persistent benefit alongside an upkeep or protection burden
* revisiting a known site or zone can produce deterministic changed-state content rather than only pure repetition
* route knowledge, tracking confidence, or access can improve through recon, specialist support, or tools
* terrain or route context affects at least one operational outcome such as access, extraction, or pursuit reliability
* targeted tests verify deterministic zone state, route access, tracking uncertainty, control effects, and reseeded-site behavior

Reconciliation update

Fold liminal hidden-domain geography into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some hidden domains may exist as adjacent layers overlapping ordinary geography, reachable only through thin-boundary locations, threshold objects, or special spatial conditions rather than free universal travel
* access may depend on narrow liminal states such as crossroads, water edges, standing stones, twilight-equivalent timing, invitation permission, or other condition-specific gates
* landscape features may function as legal or ritual boundary markers that define jurisdiction, safe passage, or exposure rather than only terrain flavor
* recurring calendar windows, seasonal openings, and moving anomaly fronts may alter access, danger, or route validity for a region on predictable schedules
* some hidden domains may desynchronize time passage relative to ordinary space, producing bounded return-time discrepancy as a domain property rather than one-off story text
* surface streets, shoreline bands, docks, ferry points, causeways, watchlines, river channels, submerged approaches, and seafloor landmarks should be able to coexist in one operational map stack when they function as one linked settlement region
* map-visible harbor control, estuary channels, perimeter defenses, and submerged access routes should carry real routing and tactical meaning rather than decorative geography only

This keeps adjacent hidden-world layering, threshold-gated access, legal terrain markers, calendar openings, roaming regional fronts, cross-layer time desynchronization, linked surface-to-submerged map stacks, and strategic geography inside the regional and hidden-geometry boundary.

- Updated: 2026-04-27T08:19:56.277Z

Goal:  
Move high-coupling UI routes and feature surfaces onto deterministic projections so derived state is read from stable view-models rather than computed ad hoc across feature boundaries.

Scope:

* identify route and feature entry points that compute derived state directly from domain systems or read across unrelated feature modules
* centralize derived UI state into projection or view-model builders owned by the correct boundary
* replace unnecessary feature-to-feature reads with orchestration-owned inputs or projection dependencies
* prioritize the highest-coupling feature surfaces rather than attempting a full UI rewrite
* keep hub-facing projections limited to actionable operational state rather than raw simulation noise
* ensure newer knowledge-heavy and exploration-heavy features also respect the same projection boundary

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no rewrite of all UI features
* no duplication of business logic between domain and projection layers
* keep projections read-focused rather than behavior-owning
* limit work to the highest-coupling routes and follow-on violations of the same boundary

Acceptance criteria:

* targeted UI surfaces no longer depend directly on domain systems for derived state
* touched routes and features read derived state through centralized projections or view-models
* unnecessary feature-to-feature coupling is removed in the targeted areas
* hub-facing projections expose bounded actionable state rather than raw simulation noise
* projection and integration tests cover the updated layer boundaries

### SPE-42 - Operative niche protection and role identity
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-25T20:43:35.580Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Strengthen operative role identity so specialist lanes solve meaningfully different problems and mixed teams carry readable tradeoffs instead of collapsing into universal competence.

Scope:

* define distinct operative niches across recruitment, training, loadouts, scouting, deployment, and recovery-facing systems
* make specialist lanes materially better than generic substitutes at specific problem classes
* support persistent role continuity across turnover without overfitting to a rigid seat model
* support hybrid or cross-trained paths only with explicit breadth-versus-depth tradeoffs, slower progression, or reduced ceilings
* reinforce identity through verb-first progression, permissions, thresholds, and gated specialist advancement rather than flat stat inflation alone
* support rare elite or doctrine-bound lanes whose strength is offset by conduct constraints, legitimacy cost, or qualification gates
* expose missing-role weaknesses and specialist contribution through existing reporting and explanation surfaces

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no class explosion
* no freeform mutation catalog
* no hard lockouts that invalidate most team compositions
* prefer bounded strengths, weaknesses, and role-specific permissions over sprawling ability catalogs

Acceptance criteria:

* at least three operative niches have clear mechanical identities with distinct problem domains
* replacing a specialist with a generic substitute creates understandable tradeoffs in at least two downstream systems
* hybrid paths remain viable but visibly less efficient than true specialization in at least one dimension
* progression unlocks at least one new verb, permission, or threshold-gated role capability rather than only numeric gain
* reports make role fit, missing-role weakness, and specialist contribution legible
* targeted tests cover niche identity, hybrid penalties, threshold unlocks, and anti-homogenization behavior

### SPE-41 - Tags, Conditions, & Graded Outcome Framework
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Done
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T11:23:52.243Z

#### Description
Status note:  
The original framework pass is complete. Follow-on scope added later is still open and should be treated as pending work on this issue.

Goal:  
Add a compact reusable rules layer for tags, bounded conditions, graded outcomes, contested resolution, bounded modifier handling, interaction-specific vulnerabilities, and information-distortion states so anomalies, missions, operatives, gear, hazards, incidents, and intel flows can interact through shared mechanics instead of one-off special cases.

Scope:

* define reusable canonical tags for operative roles, gear properties, anomaly behaviors, mission requirements, environmental factors, hazard traits, and system hooks where appropriate
* add a compact condition set for teams, operatives, anomalies, hazards, or agency state using bounded mechanical effects rather than sprawling bespoke statuses
* support graded outcomes so key resolutions can land in worse-than-expected, partial, expected, or strong-result bands instead of simple binary success/failure
* add bounded contested-resolution patterns for agency versus anomaly, team versus concealment, rival pressure, recovery pressure, or other opposed situations where the simulation benefits from explicit contest logic
* add bounded modifier handling so positive and negative modifiers can be combined through a consistent shared rule rather than per-system special cases
* support exceptional high-volatility outcome bands only where anomaly-facing or extraordinary incident content needs more memorable outcomes without making the default simulation loop swingy everywhere
* support explicit vulnerability, susceptibility, countermeasure, and interaction rules so tools, containment methods, environments, and anomaly traits can interact through shared tags and bounded outcome logic
* support shared distortion-state tags such as misleading intel, unreliable contract state, or internal fragmentation where upstream systems need canonical propagation markers
* integrate the framework into existing readiness, routing, scouting, incident, recovery, and report/explanation systems where it improves clarity
* keep the framework deterministic and compact enough to reason about in validation harnesses

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no giant keyword soup
* no proliferating one-off status effects with unclear overlap
* no broad randomness layer across the default simulation loop
* prefer small reusable vocabularies over subsystem-specific jargon
* reuse existing visibility and explanation helpers so tags, conditions, modifier states, contests, vulnerability rules, distortion states, and outcome bands remain legible

Acceptance criteria:

* a canonical tag architecture exists and is used by at least three existing systems
* a compact condition set exists with clearly bounded mechanical effects
* at least one major resolution path uses graded outcome bands instead of binary pass/fail only
* bounded modifier handling exists as a shared rule used across more than one system
* at least one contested-resolution path is represented through the shared framework
* at least one vulnerability/countermeasure interaction family is represented through shared tags and bounded outcome logic
* at least one shared distortion-state family can propagate across more than one system
* targeted tests and validation scenarios cover tag interactions, condition effects, modifier handling, contested resolution, vulnerability rules, distortion states, and graded outcomes deterministically

Reconciliation update

Fold severe-hit family tables and mixed critical outcomes into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* severe outcomes may be routed through attack-family-specific result tables or families rather than one universal critical path when that preserves meaningful differences between projectile, impact, occult, medical, or other domains
* high-severity resolution should combine direct loss with typed conditions, body-region consequences, disruption states, and longer-tail fallout instead of flat bonus damage only
* severity should escalate through clear bands from inconvenience to disabling harm to catastrophic loss, staying compact enough to remain explainable and testable

This keeps family-specific critical tables, mixed damage-plus-condition outcomes, and escalation-band severe effects inside the shared rules framework.

Reconciliation update

Fold projected influence fields, multi-vector contaminant delivery, and scripted invulnerability semantics into this issue.

Implementation emphasis:

* some control effects should project passively as ambient fields, with different outcomes by target stance, distance band, or target tier, rather than always resolving as one explicit cast against one target
* one lethal or contaminant effect family may be authorable across several delivery channels such as touch, bite, injected payload, breath, spray, mist, cloud, residue, or medium-borne spread while still sharing one downstream consequence ladder
* invulnerability should remain distinct from flat immunity by supporting bespoke true-kill conditions, incomplete-defeat regeneration, and method-sensitive neutralization rules that other systems can query
* resistance should continue to resolve through typed matrices and channel-specific protection rather than one generic universal defense bucket

This keeps passive control auras, shared multi-channel contamination families, script-based immortality, and typed resistance semantics inside the shared rules framework.

#### Relations
- related: SPE-42 Operative niche protection and role identity
- related: SPE-52 Faction Agenda & Internal Cohesion Layer

- Status: Backlog

- Labels: simulation, system, core-loop
- Updated: 2026-04-26T05:17:28.624Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement the bounded operational action budget so preparation, response, logistics, and push-deeper choices compete for scarce agency capacity.

Scope:

* define a canonical action budget for at least one recurring decision phase
* require preparation, staffing, intel, procurement, recovery, traversal, extraction, and response choices to compete for that shared budget
* support bounded retries, push-deeper choices, and improvised high-leverage actions with explicit upside and explicit downstream cost
* support posture changes, mode switches, burden-to-throughput coupling, maintained effects, and overdraw as part of the same action economy where relevant
* support mission-layer objective conflict where extraction, containment, intelligence, survival, suppression, or evacuation can compete rather than align automatically
* expose action use, deferred choices, and foregone alternatives through existing summaries and reports

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no turn-based combat layer
* no click-heavy scheduling puzzle
* no endless gambler loop for retries
* prefer a small number of high-leverage action slots over sprawling task lists

Acceptance criteria:

* at least three existing systems compete for one shared operational budget
* at least one bounded retry or push-deeper path exists with explicit downstream cost
* at least one logistics or sustainment constraint limits available choices materially
* at least one retreat, abandonment, pursuit, or extraction branch carries explicit tradeoffs
* players can see what was committed, what was deferred, and why outcomes changed
* targeted tests cover deterministic allocation, contention, escalation, and downstream impact

Implement the relative-agency competition layer so campaign progress is measured not only against internal readiness and escalation, but also against external standings, rival organizations, comparative pressure, shifting external trust, and operational legitimacy.

* add canonical agency standing/ranking state that can rise or fall over time
* represent rival or peer organizations abstractly rather than as fully simulated squads
* support bounded comparative pressure on contracts, reputation, payouts, intel access, staffing attractiveness, or escalation consequences where appropriate
* expose standing shifts and rival pressure through existing campaign summaries and player-facing report surfaces
* support bounded trust, reaction, or reliability shifts for external actors such as contacts, sponsors, intermediaries, or temporary partners where agency posture should affect access or cooperation
* split legitimacy into institutional legitimacy and operational cover level so action can be gated not only by status but by whether an operation is legally sanctioned, tolerated, deniable, or unsanctioned
* connect legitimacy and cover level to mission availability, response type, access promises, and fallout routing where relevant
* keep the system deterministic and tightly integrated with existing escalation, faction, contract, and pressure systems

Constraints:

* no freeform narrative rivalry engine
* prefer bounded comparative metrics, pressure hooks, legitimacy states, and event summaries over per-rival tactical simulation
* reuse existing campaign-state, faction, contract, and pressure systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* agency standing exists as a distinct canonical layer in the simulation
* rival pressure affects at least two existing downstream systems or decision surfaces
* at least one external-trust or cooperation variable is influenced procedurally by agency state or pressure
* at least one operation or decision path is affected by institutional legitimacy or operational cover level

- Labels: simulation, core-loop, system
- Updated: 2026-04-13T03:55:51.910Z

#### Description
Goal:
Build the player-facing procurement surface on top of the implemented funding, procurement backlog, and budget-pressure systems.

Scope:

* present current procurement options, costs, blockers, and pending backlog state
* show budget impact and bounded pressure consequences using existing simulation outputs
* expose canonical requirement and affordability blockers clearly
* support purchase/request actions through the existing procurement path where available

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* reuse the existing funding/procurement system
* no economy redesign
* no broad UI overhaul outside the bounded market screen
* no duplicate inventory/procurement subsystem

Acceptance criteria:

* the player can inspect procurement choices and their budget impact
* affordability and gating blockers are visible and deterministic
* pending procurement state is visible without debug-only views
* the screen does not introduce a parallel procurement model

### SPE-31 - Operations hub opportunity surface
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, core-loop, system
- Updated: 2026-04-22T13:23:15.895Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement the operations hub as the bounded home screen for campaign management, routing, and simulation-driven opportunity surfacing.

Scope:

* provide a single player-facing hub for current cycle state, active pressures, and key organizational summaries
* link into existing operational flows such as contracts, readiness, intel, staffing, procurement, recovery, and reports without duplicating their logic
* surface bounded opportunities such as rumors, leads, visitors, or filtered alerts derived from existing simulation state
* expose only actionable information needed for decision-making and suppress raw systemic noise
* project knowledge, exploration, and follow-on presentation scope through summaries rather than computing new simulation in the hub layer
* keep the hub suitable as the canonical campaign entry surface rather than a broader civic sandbox

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no new simulation subsystems inside the hub
* no broad UI redesign outside the bounded hub shell
* no large persisted view-state model
* prefer projection and summary surfaces over raw system reads

Acceptance criteria:

* the player has one bounded operations home screen
* the hub reflects current simulation state through existing helpers and summaries
* the hub links into already-supported operational flows without duplicating logic
* at least one simulation-driven opportunity, rumor, or lead is surfaced through the hub
* the hub remains a presentation and routing surface rather than a separate simulation layer

### SPE-17 - Execution Instability on Readiness-Time Scaffold
- Team: SpectraNoir
- Status: Backlog
- Priority: 2
- Labels: simulation, core-loop, system
- Updated: 2026-04-23T06:07:10.824Z

#### Description
Goal:  
Implement bounded execution-instability rules on top of the completed readiness and time-cost scaffold so upstream distortion can change mission flow without reopening the base timing foundation.

Scope:

* treat the readiness and time-cost scaffold as already implemented foundation and do not re-specify it here
* support explicit instability flags such as shifting objectives, unreliable allies, and bounded mid-mission state flips
* require those instability effects to be triggered by upstream institutional, intel, or contract state rather than free-floating randomness
* keep shared exploration, traversal, inspection, combat, and extraction actions on the same operational clock while instability modifies outcomes on that clock
* surface instability cause and downstream effect through existing execution summaries or report output

Constraints:

* deterministic only
* no rewrite of the base readiness/time-cost scaffold
* no broad chaos layer or freeform surprise generator
* prefer a small set of explicit instability flags and consequences over bespoke scenario exceptions
* reuse existing readiness, time-cost, knowledge, contract, and reporting systems where possible

Acceptance criteria:

* at least one execution flow applies a bounded instability flag on top of the existing readiness/time-cost scaffold
* at least one instability source changes objective state, ally reliability, or mission routing during execution
* mixed exploration/combat time accounting remains on one shared clock while instability is active
* player-facing output identifies both the upstream cause and the downstream execution effect
* targeted tests cover deterministic instability triggering, execution-state change, and surfaced output

#### Relations
- blocks: SPE-284 Combat with a parallel objective track
- blocks: SPE-281 Cross-Session Attrition Persistence
- blocks: SPE-282 Momentum Rewards from Sustained Deployment

---
